intitule: Reading Comprehension: Climate Change and Agriculture

enonce.texte:
Read the following text carefully and answer the questions below.

The Impact of Climate Change on African Farmers

Climate change is having a severe impact on agriculture across Africa. In recent years, farmers have been facing unpredictable seasons, with rainfall patterns becoming increasingly erratic. In many regions, the rainy season will be getting shorter, while droughts will be lasting longer. This has led to significant crop failures and food insecurity.

For example, in the Sahel region, farmers have been struggling to adapt to the changing climate. They report that the traditional farming calendar no longer works. In some areas, the rains that used to arrive in May are now coming in June or July. This shift means that the growing season will be shrinking, forcing farmers to use new techniques and drought-resistant seeds.

Experts warn that if current trends continue, agricultural production in Africa could decrease by up to 20% by 2050. This would have devastating consequences for millions of people who depend on farming for their livelihoods. However, many farmers are showing resilience and innovation by adopting sustainable practices and diversifying their crops.

questions.texte: According to the text, what are the main consequences of climate change on agriculture in Africa?
questions.reponse: The main consequences are unpredictable seasons, erratic rainfall patterns, shorter rainy seasons, longer droughts, crop failures, and food insecurity.

questions.texte: Explain how the changing rainfall patterns are affecting farmers in the Sahel region.
questions.reponse: In the Sahel region, rains that used to arrive in May are now coming in June or July. This means the growing season is getting shorter, forcing farmers to use new techniques and drought-resistant seeds.

questions.texte: What does the author suggest about the future of African agriculture?
questions.reponse: The author suggests that if current trends continue, agricultural production could decrease by up to 20% by 2050. However, farmers are showing resilience by adopting sustainable practices and diversifying their crops.

enonce.texte: Match the words from the text with their correct definitions.

questions.texte: Match the following terms with their definitions:

sousQuestions.texte: Climate change
sousQuestions.reponse: Long-term changes in temperature and weather patterns

sousQuestions.texte: Drought
Crops
sousQuestions.reponse: Plants grown for food or commercial purposes

sousQuestions.texte: Sustainable
sousQuestions.reponse: Using methods that do not harm the environment

sousQuestions.texte: Resilience
sousQuestions.reponse: The ability to recover quickly from difficulties

enonce.texte:
The Future Continuous is used to talk about actions that will be in progress at a specific time in the future.

Structure: Subject + will be + verb-ing

Example: "The rainy season will be getting shorter."

questions.texte: Complete the following sentences using the Future Continuous form of the verbs in brackets.

sousQuestions.texte: By 2050, farmers in the Sahel _______________ (use) more drought-resistant seeds.
The average temperature _______________ (rise) significantly in the coming decades.
Many rural communities _______________ (struggle) to find enough water for their crops.
Experts _______________ (monitor) the situation closely.
The government _______________ (implement) new agricultural policies next year.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using the Future Continuous tense.

questions.texte: Rewrite the sentences using the Future Continuous tense.

sousQuestions.texte: The farmers will adapt to the new climate conditions. (emphasize the ongoing nature)
sousQuestions.reponse: The farmers will be adapting to the new climate conditions.

sousQuestions.texte: The government will provide support to affected communities. (emphasize the ongoing nature)
sousQuestions.reponse: The government will be providing support to affected communities.

sousQuestions.texte: Scientists will study the impact of climate change on agriculture. (emphasize the ongoing nature)
sousQuestions.reponse: Scientists will be studying the impact of climate change on agriculture.

sousQuestions.texte: Rainfall patterns will change in most regions. (emphasize the ongoing nature)
sousQuestions.reponse: Rainfall patterns will be changing in most regions.

enonce.texte:
Read the following situation and make predictions using the Future Continuous.

Situation: Climate change is affecting agriculture in Africa. In the next 10 years, many changes will occur.

questions.texte: Make predictions about the future of agriculture using the Future Continuous tense.
questions.reponse: Accept any logical answer using the Future Continuous.

sousQuestions.texte: What will farmers be doing to adapt to unpredictable seasons?
sousQuestions.reponse: Farmers will be using new farming techniques and drought-resistant seeds.

sousQuestions.texte: What will governments be doing to help farmers?
sousQuestions.reponse: Governments will be implementing new agricultural policies and providing support.

sousQuestions.texte: What will scientists be doing to find solutions?
sousQuestions.reponse: Scientists will be studying the impact of climate change and developing new technologies.

enonce.texte:
Complete the following sentences with the appropriate words from the box.

Word Box: climate change, seasons, rainfall, crops, drought, resilient, predict

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct words from the box.

sousQuestions.texte: _____________ is one of the biggest challenges facing farmers today.
The _____________ are becoming more unpredictable, making it difficult to plan for the growing season.
In some regions, _____________ has decreased significantly, leading to water shortages.
Many farmers are losing their _____________ due to the prolonged _____________.
It is difficult to _____________ exactly when the rains will come.
Despite the challenges, farmers are showing their _____________ by finding new ways to grow food.

enonce.texte: Read the following questions and write a paragraph discussing potential solutions to the problems caused by climate change in agriculture.

questions.texte: In a well-structured paragraph, discuss the possible solutions to the challenges farmers are facing due to climate change.
questions.reponse: Students should write a paragraph that includes: introduction of the problem, explanation of solutions (e.g., using drought-resistant seeds, adopting sustainable practices, government support, education), and a concluding thought.

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are a journalist. Write a short article for your local newspaper about the impact of climate change on farmers in your region. Use the vocabulary and the Future Continuous tense to describe what will be happening in the future.

Include:

  • A title for your article.
  • A description of the current situation.
  • Predictions for the future using the Future Continuous.
  • Suggestions for solutions.

questions.texte: Write an article about the impact of climate change on local farmers.
questions.reponse: Students should write an article with a title, a description of the situation, predictions using the Future Continuous, and suggestions for solutions.
